source: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §3 known limitations + post-T-024 gap analysis
title: Memory layer completion: L1 TTL, L3 dedup, L1->L2 distillation, row sequencing
what_failed: Four gaps remain after T-024. (1) L1 grows forever — no 30-day TTL enforcement. (2) L3 accumulates duplicates — if Claude re-writes the same fact across sessions, it stacks. (3) No L1->L2 distillation — L1 is a write-only archive; memorable facts never flow automatically to L2 for future recall. (4) All rows in a log_turn() call share the same timestamp — within-turn ordering is insertion-order-only, which is fragile when Supabase returns rows in an unspecified order.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py: memory_write(tier='l3') has no duplicate check; log_turn() has no seq field; no prune_l1() method; no get_l1_thread() method. agent/session.py: on_exit() never distills or prunes. memory/ module: does not exist.
why_likely: L1 TTL was always 'planned, not yet enforced' per architecture doc. L3 dedup was never designed. Distillation is the largest gap — it is the bridge between raw archive and useful recall, and the prerequisite for Phase B autonomy.
reproduction: Run Pi for several sessions discussing the same preferences. Query l3_active_memory — duplicate entries accumulate. Query raw_wiki after 30 days — no entries ever deleted. Ask Pi about something from a past session that was never explicitly memory_write'd — not found, because L1->L2 distillation never ran.
expected: L1 pruned every session, L3 deduplicated on write, session facts distilled to L2 on exit, row ordering stable via seq field.
actual: L1 grows unbounded, L3 accumulates duplicates, L1 is a dead archive with no downstream pipeline, row order relies on insertion order.
suggested_fix: See implementation notes — memory/pipeline.py, additions to tools/tools_memory.py, agent/session.py on_exit update.
fix_summary: Added _is_l3_duplicate() (SQLite prefix check, 120 chars, same category), get_l1_thread() (fetches and sorts by turn+seq metadata), prune_l1(days=30) (deletes old raw_wiki rows) to MemoryTools. Created memory/pipeline.py with distill_session() — reads L1 thread via Groq (free), extracts importance>=4 facts, writes to L2. agent/session.py on_exit() now calls distill_session then prune_l1, both non-fatal. seq field added to log_turn() rows for stable within-turn ordering.

verification.result: 11 tests passed (offline stubs): format, extract, distill_session happy/sad paths, dry_run, Groq failure handling
